mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Provides static utility methods for OSS emulator operations including printer enrollment setup, configuration, and flow management
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This helper provides methods for setting up printer enrollment through the OSS emulator, which can be used to fulfill the precondition of enrolling a printer with a specific plan tier. The setup_oss_emulator method handles complete printer configuration including plan selection.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow by calling multiple helper methods in sequence: access emulator, sign in, select 3-month trial, fill printer configuration, select app type, and start flow
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This composite method can be used to enroll a printer with a specific plan through the OSS emulator, fulfilling the precondition requirement. It handles the complete enrollment flow including printer configuration and plan selection.

precondition_mapping.notes: OSS emulator helper provides printer enrollment capabilities, but the test case requires enrollment with a 50+ pages plan specifically. The helper supports plan configuration but may need additional configuration for plan tier selection. Full enrollment flow may require additional components from other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No methods found in this batch for verifying or ensuring subscription has no free months. This likely requires API helpers or subscription management utilities that may exist in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No methods found in this batch for verifying or setting subscription status. This likely requires API helpers or subscription state management utilities that may exist in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No methods found in this batch for pausing a plan. The landing_page_helper.py has methods for landing page navigation but not for pause plan functionality. Pause plan methods likely exist in other batches containing plan management or dashboard helpers.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Clicks billing cycle dropdown, selects first previous option, and verifies the title changed.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 2 and 5 involve checking the Billing Cycle Period card and verifying plan pause information display. This method verifies billing cycle period changes, which is related to the billing cycle card verification required in the test case.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period by index from the dropdown. Opens dropdown, clicks the appropriate option (1, 2, or 3), and verifies the table updated.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method can be used to select different billing cycle periods for verification, which is relevant to steps 2 and 5 where the billing cycle card needs to be checked at different states.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the expected total pages printed. Extracts numbers from the total pages printed element and validates against expected value.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 19 requires checking total pages printed info and verifying the correct number (15 pages) is displayed. This method directly validates the total pages printed value in the billing table.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Validates total pages printed, plan pages, rollover pages, additional pages, plan price, overage price, previous billing, charges, tax, and total.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Multiple steps require verifying specific values in the billing section: step 8 verifies complimentary pages value (0 of 10), step 11 verifies updated value (6 of 10), step 14 verifies additional pages value (5 of 10), step 18 verifies full complimentary pages (10 of 10), and step 19 verifies total pages (15). This comprehensive method can validate all these billing section fields.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and selecting each cycle. For each cycle, opens dropdown, selects the option, waits for table update, and validates all fields.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 2 and 5 involve checking the billing cycle card at different states (before and after time shift). This method can validate billing data across different billing cycles, which is relevant to verifying the card state changes.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Validates the print history page for printers without pens by checking which elements are visible and which are not. Verifies print history section and page title are visible, while detailed billing elements are not visible.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 involve navigating to the Print and Payment History page. This method validates that the print history page loaded correctly by checking for expected visible elements, which can be used to verify successful navigation.

matched_methods.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the print history page shows the expected price and page count. Extracts numbers from plan price text and validates both price and page count.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 8 and 9 involve verifying complimentary pages value and information message which includes plan details. This method validates plan information including page counts, which is relevant to verifying the plan details displayed on the billing cycle card.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value on the print history page matches the expected value. Extracts numbers from current total text and validates against expected value.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 19 involves checking total pages printed info. This method verifies the current total value displayed, which is related to the total pages verification required in step 19.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including verifying billing cycle periods, selecting billing cycle options, verifying billing table data, normalizing and validating currency values, verifying billing section details with expected values, validating all billing cycles data, validating FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications, verifying plan information, and verifying current total values
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper is HIGHLY RELEVANT to the test case as it provides comprehensive methods for verifying billing cycle periods, billing table data, billing section details, plan information, and page counts - all of which are core requirements of the Billing Cycle Period card test case. Multiple steps require verification of billing data, page counts, and billing cycle information.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Helper module for OSS emulator operations including accessing the emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, handling OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, handling dynamic security notices, value propositions, printer replacement, HP Plus activation, country selection, and verification of reminder checkboxes and trial text
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper provides methods for printer enrollment through the OSS emulator, which is relevant to precondition 0 (enrolling printer with 50+ pages plan). The setup_oss_emulator method handles complete printer configuration including plan selection.

assertion_methods.name: validate_no_pens_print_history_page
assertion_methods.relevance: This method validates that the print history page loaded correctly by checking for expected visible elements (print_history_section, page_title). Can be used to verify successful navigation to the Print and Payment History page.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: Navigation method to Print and Payment History page not found in this batch. The validate_no_pens_print_history_page method can verify the page loaded but does not perform the navigation itself. Navigation methods likely exist in other batches containing page objects or navigation helpers.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: Batch 9 contains HIGHLY RELEVANT components for this test case, particularly the PrintHistoryHelper which provides comprehensive methods for verifying billing cycle periods, billing table data, billing section details, plan information, and page counts. However, this batch is missing critical components including: (1) Page objects for PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage with locators for billing cycle card elements, progress bars, tooltips, and info icons, (2) Navigation methods to access the Print and Payment History page, (3) Time manipulation utilities for the 32-day shift, (4) Print job simulation API or utilities, (5) Pause plan functionality, (6) Progress bar color verification methods, (7) Tooltip interaction methods, (8) Visual regression testing utilities, (9) Responsive testing utilities. The PrintHistoryHelper provides excellent verification capabilities for billing data and page counts (steps 8, 11, 14, 18, 19) but cannot handle the UI interactions, navigation, backend operations, or visual/responsive testing required by this test case. These missing components likely exist in other batches containing page objects, navigation helpers, API utilities, and visual testing tools.
